events.title: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
events.summary: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.

events.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
events.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

events.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
events.summary: Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham".

events.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
events.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

events.title: Recovery of Ebola patients offers rare moments of joy at epicentre of outbreak
events.summary: There are glimpses of happiness in the Democratic Republic of Congo's fight against the virus that has killed more than 170.

events.title: 'They came with machetes' - deadline looms for migrants to leave South Africa
events.summary: Protesters have set 30 June as the date for all undocumented migrants to leave the country.

events.title: Kenyans mark two years since Gen Z protests
events.summary: Two years on, families of those killed during Kenya’s 2024 anti-government protests returned to Parliament to remember their loved ones and demand justice.

events.title: Heavy security deployed in South Africa ahead of anti-migrant protests
events.summary: Thousands of people have left South Africa ahead of Tuesday's deadline set by anti-migrant groups.

events.title: Most Venezuelan city in the US rallies to help after quakes
events.summary: In Doral, where more than 40% of residents are of Venezuelan origin, the community has come together to send aid to the country after it was hit by earthquakes.

events.title: Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes
events.summary: Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people.

events.title: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
events.summary: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.

events.title: I hear my son crying beneath the rubble, says Venezuela earthquake survivor
events.summary: Andreina Valerio says she fears for her son, who is trapped with her partner and in-laws beneath a collapsed building.

events.title: Anger directed at Venezuela's official response to earthquake recovery
events.summary: Residents in quake-affected areas have expressed anger over the state's efforts to locate survivors and help victims, the BBC's Orla Guerin reports.

events.title: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
events.summary: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.

events.title: Supreme Court allows late-arriving mail-in ballots in defeat for Trump
events.summary: The decision could affect voting deadlines of more than a dozen states that will be pivotal in deciding the control of Congress after the midterm elections.

events.title: What to know about the looming  deadline on North American free trade
events.summary: The US, Canada and Mexico were to reach an agreement on whether to extend their free trade pact by 1 July. All signs point to them blowing past that deadline.

events.title: Angry Venezuelans accuse government of negligence over earthquake response
events.summary: People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.

events.title: Aftershock frays nerves as many Venezuelans left to fend for themselves
events.summary: In areas devastated by the twin earthquakes, people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.

events.title: Philippines bans video game played by alleged high school shooter
events.summary: The rare school shooting that left three students killed and 20 others injured has shocked the country.

events.title: Australian man charged with murder after dead girl found in suitcase in Thailand
events.summary: The body of Tunchanok Donhomla, 17, was found discarded near a railway track on Saturday, police say.

events.title: Pakistani strikes kill at least 28 civilians in Afghanistan, UN says
events.summary: Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".

events.title: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
events.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

events.title: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
events.summary: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.

events.title: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
events.summary: The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said.

events.title: Putin makes rare admission of fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian strikes
events.summary: The Russian president acknowledged Ukraine's attacks were "obviously creating problems" but denied the shortages were "critical".

events.title: Six people shot dead at centre for mothers and children in Germany
events.summary: The male suspect who has been arrested was in a custody dispute over his baby daughter, police say.

events.title: What do we know about the shooting in Stade, Germany?
events.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker reports from the scene of the shooting that killed six people in northern Germany.

events.title: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
events.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

events.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
events.summary: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

events.title: Trump accuses Iran of ceasefire breach after Strait of Hormuz attack
events.summary: A Singapore-flagged ship was struck crossing the waterway on Thursday, prompting a large-scale evacuation to be paused.

events.title: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
events.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

events.title: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
events.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.

events.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
events.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

events.title: DR Congo bans mass gatherings in the  capital to prevent spread of Ebola
events.summary: Opposition politicians accuse the government of using the outbreak to halt a planned protest.

events.title: Leading Pakistan activist given life sentence over soldier's killing at rally
events.summary: Human rights campaigner Mahrang Baloch denied inciting protesters before the official's death.

events.title: Africa's greatest World Cup kits - pick your favourite
events.summary: Your chance to rank 10 of the most colourful and iconic kits African teams have worn at the World Cup.

events.title: Twins marry twins in joyous Nigerian joint wedding
events.summary: Brothers Taiwo and Kehinde Oguntoye speak to the BBC after getting hitched to twin sisters.

events.title: Watch: Football fans celebrate in Ghana after draw with England
events.summary: Ghana correspondent Thomas Naadi reports from Black Star Square in Accra.

events.title: Celebrations as South Africa reach World Cup knockout stages for the first time
events.summary: South Africa reaches World Cup knockout round for first time with 1-0 win over South Korea.

events.title: 'We could call it racist' - Ivory Coast boss sad at Schweinsteiger comments
events.summary: Ivory Coast boss Emerse Fae says comments from ex-Germany midfielder Bastian Schweinsteiger about their "wild" playing style could be considered racist.

events.title: The woman who fought for Pakistan's disappeared men now faces life in jail
events.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize nominee has spent years demanding answers about the missing in Balochistan province.

events.title: A Chinese box office hit sparks a debate about identity in Singapore
events.summary: A nostalgic tale about family, hope and hardship has opened an unexpected conversation.

events.title: The Bhojpuri singers fighting vulgar tag on one of India's oldest languages
events.summary: Through radically different sounds, two musicians are reshaping how Bhojpuri is heard and understood in their country.

events.title: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
events.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

events.title: What news reports from 1600s tell us about life in Mughal India
events.summary: Thousands of forgotten news reports are reshaping our understanding of Aurangzeb and the Mughal world.

events.title: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
events.summary: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

events.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave
events.summary: France has issued red heatwave alerts for around half the country as temperatures soar to record levels.

events.title: Temperatures hit record levels in western Europe
events.summary: France, Spain and Italy, have been hardest hit by the heatwave so far.

events.title: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
events.summary: France is being forced to re-think its longstanding reservations about air-conditioning as temperatures soar.

events.title: Women alleging rape and sexual assault in France call to abolish statute of limitations
events.summary: More than 50 women are calling for the abolition of the statute of limitations which they say has prevented them seeking justice.

events.title: Perry and Gardner dominate India to get World Cup revenge
events.summary: A powerful partnership by Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carries Australia to the greatest run chase in Women's T20 World Cup history, knocking India out of the tournament.

events.title: Maroons recall veteran for Origin decider after Blues' bombshell
events.summary: Billy Slater names Patrick Carrigan in his Queensland squad for State of Origin III despite the Broncos forward having not played since late May due to an ankle injury.

events.title: Les Mills, NZ Olympian and founder of global fitness brand, dies aged 91
events.summary: The New Zealand Olympian who opened an Auckland gym in 1968 that grew into an international group fitness brand has died aged 91.

events.title: How a handshake may decide Slater's Maroons coaching future
events.summary: Billy Slater says he will not sign another contract to be Queensland State of Origin coach, but that does not mean he will not continue in the role.

events.title: Australian Kasatkina finally wins on grass to kick off Wimbledon run
events.summary: After a winless grass-court swing, Australian Daria Kasatkina becomes Australia's first winner at Wimbledon for 2026.

events.title: How a punt on a trial in Italy created the Socceroos' next star
events.summary: Cristian Volpato was rejected by two Australian soccer academies. His family dropped everything to go for a trial in Italy. That was the making of Australia's next star.

events.title: Dismissal of lawsuit against author Neil Gaiman upheld, court says case should be heard in New Zealand
events.summary: Neil Gaiman was sued by a Kiwi woman who alleged she was sexually assaulted by the author multiple times on Waiheke Island.

events.title: North-west Victorian sporting league adopts new concussion app
events.summary: A sporting league in regional Victoria is the first to adopt a concussion app aimed at improving communication and accountability around concussions at grassroots sports.
